title: Lori Duffy
content:
Senior Counsel | Lead Private Wealth, Estates and Trusts
People.ContactEmail lduffy@mccarthy.ca
- University of Ottawa
Lori Duffy is a senior Trusts and Estates advisor in our leading Tax Group, based in Toronto and leads the firm’s Private Wealth, Estates and Trusts practice.
With more than three decades of experience advising on all aspects of wills, trusts, and estate planning, Lori has earned a reputation as one of Canada’s leading Estate and Personal Tax Planning experts. A testament to her deep knowledge and excellent client service, Lori has been named one of the top lawyers in Canada by Best Lawyers in her practice area, and is repeatedly recognized as a leading practitioner in Estate & Personal Tax Planning in the Canadian Legal Lexpert Directory.
A strategic advisor known for her innovative approach, Lori advises clients in managing and transferring their assets through the use of business succession plans, trusts and wills. Her wide-ranging practice also includes all areas of trusts, wills and estate administration, including work under the Substitute Decisions Act.
Lori is fully immersed in the wills, trusts, and estate planning ecosystem. She has authored a thought leadership newsletter and is the Ontario Editor of the Estate & Trust Reports (Carswell). Lori was also co-editor of the Ontario Bar Association Trusts and Estates section newsletter, and was the editor of Without Prejudice, the Women’s Law Association newsletter (1989-91). She is a member of the Society of Trust and Estate Practitioners (STEP), a director of the Martin Family Initiative, a member of the Advisory Task Force on Planned Giving for St. Michael’s Hospital Foundation, and is now working with Campfire Circle to develop its Planned Giving Advisory Committee. Lori is also a former Adjunct Professor at University of Toronto Law School teaching the Wills and Estates law course. She has been a seminar instructor at the Bar Admission course and has lectured in many seminars for the Canadian Bar Association, the Ontario Bar Association, the Law Society of Ontario, The Canadian Institute and the International Bar Association.
Lori is also a strong advocate for the advancement of women in the legal profession and in the business world. She is a Past President of the Canadian Association of Women Executives and Entrepreneurs; and a former member of the Executive of the Women’s Law Association of Ontario.
Lori obtained her LL.B. from the University of Ottawa in 1982 and her LL.M. from Osgoode Hall Law School in 2009. She is called to the Bar in Ontario (1984). She earned an ADR Certificate of Achievement from the University of Windsor/Stitt in 2009, and she holds a BA in History from McMaster University (1979).
